notes: Public adverts avoid client names, tenant names, and exact project identifiers.
Content below uses verified employer context plus role-safe industry-standard wording.
Vacancy #65 uses Competitive pay because the supplied salary value is ambiguous and should not be guessed.
Vacancy #64 uses Project location to be confirmed because the supplied details do not confirm a public project location.
Where a field could not be filled honestly with a project-specific fact, it has been completed with a truth-safe descriptor such as Confidential programme, Competitive, or To be confirmed.

factsUsed: Project management and commercial advisory services for large-scale data centre and infrastructure developments
Support across feasibility, design, construction, commissioning, and operations
Acts on behalf of owners / developers and interfaces with contractors and specialist vendors
Trusted by data centre developers, investors, and operators across Europe, APAC, and AMERS
Supports hyperscale, colocation, and AI-enabled facility developments through commissioning and operations
Specialises in installation of HV and LV cables, data, and fiber-optic systems
Works across UK and Europe in data centre and infrastructure-related sectors
Positions itself as an electrical and data fibre installation company
Company status active
Nature of business listed as electrical installation
Incorporated on 29 March 2019
Electrical Testing & Inspection Specialists
Construction sector company
Website listed as criticaltestingservices.com

Electrical commissioning managers on data centre projects are commonly responsible for on-site commissioning activities, schedule/report management, issue resolution, H&S compliance, and handover support

Comparable Frankfurt commissioning roles reference electrical commissioning plans, QA/QC interface, client/vendor coordination, functional testing, integrated systems testing, and handover documentation
L4/L5 commissioning roles commonly include leadership, project planning, technical oversight, quality assurance, risk management, and reporting/documentation
Data centre M&E quantity surveyor roles commonly cover LV/MV package support, procurement through final account, CVRs, valuations, variations, forecasting, and contract compliance
Data centre electrical testing work is framed around uptime, electrical integrity, compliance, preventive maintenance, thermography, power quality, and troubleshooting

Mission-critical testing scopes commonly include inspections, verification, startup, switchgear, transformers, breakers, control cables, grounding/bonding, and turnover from construction to operations

jobs.title: Electrical Commissioning Manager — Data Centre — Frankfurt
jobs.overview: Electrical Commissioning Manager required for a client-side data centre programme in Frankfurt. Working from the HMJ Global network into a confidential mission-critical project team, this role focuses on electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities, coordinating stakeholders through pre-energisation, testing, systems interface management, documentation close-out, and handover readiness. It will suit someone confident leading commissioning workflows in complex data centre environments, with the ability to keep safety, quality, reporting, and milestone control aligned while multiple contractors and vendors move through commissioning phases.
responsibilities: Lead electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 stages on a live client-side data centre programme.
Coordinate contractors, specialist vendors, and delivery teams through inspections, testing, issue resolution, and turnover milestones.
Develop and manage commissioning trackers, witness plans, progress reporting, and close-out actions.
Review test packs, red-lines, commissioning records, and handover documentation for completeness and quality.
Support interface management between construction, QA/QC, commissioning, and operational stakeholders.
Drive safe systems of work and consistent quality standards through energisation, functional testing, and final handover.
requirements: Strong experience in electrical commissioning within data centre or mission-critical environments.
Working knowledge of structured L1-L5 commissioning activities and close-out processes.
Confident coordinating multiple stakeholders in a client-side or owner’s representative setting.
Able to manage commissioning documentation, issue tracking, reporting, and turnover readiness.
Good understanding of critical electrical systems such as LV/MV distribution, switchgear, UPS, generators, or associated support systems.
Strong communication skills with a practical, delivery-focused approach.

shareSpec.overview: HMJ Global is supporting the search for an Electrical Commissioning Manager to join a confidential client-side data centre programme in Frankfurt. This opportunity is focused on leading electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities in a mission-critical environment, ensuring systems progress in a controlled, safe, and well-documented manner from installation readiness through to energisation and final handover. The successful candidate will be comfortable coordinating contractors, vendors, and delivery stakeholders while maintaining clear reporting lines, close-out discipline, and a strong focus on programme milestones. This is well suited to a commissioning professional who understands how to drive quality and turnover readiness in complex data centre projects without losing sight of safety, interface management, and operational handover standards.
responsibilities: Lead electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities.
Coordinate testing, inspections, issue resolution, and turnover actions with contractors and vendors.
Manage commissioning plans, trackers, witness points, and reporting outputs.
Review commissioning packs, red-lines, and handover records for quality and completeness.
Support interface alignment between construction, QA/QC, commissioning, and operations.
Maintain strong safety and quality standards through energisation and final handover.
requirements: Proven electrical commissioning experience in data centre or mission-critical delivery.
Strong understanding of L1-L5 commissioning structure and close-out processes.
Comfortable leading stakeholders in a client-side or owner’s representative environment.
Good knowledge of critical electrical infrastructure and commissioning documentation.
Clear communicator with a practical, organised, delivery-led approach.

Working within a confidential mission-critical project environment, the role centres on electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities, helping drive testing, stakeholder coordination, documentation close-out, and handover readiness. It will suit a commissioning professional who can maintain momentum across multiple interfaces while keeping safety, quality, reporting, and close-out standards under control in a fast-moving data centre setting.
responsibilities: Lead electrical commissioning delivery across L1-L5 activities on a live client-side programme.
Coordinate contractors, specialist vendors, and delivery teams through testing, issue resolution, and close-out phases.
Monitor commissioning progress, witness activities, and turnover actions against project milestones.
Review test documentation, commissioning records, red-lines, and handover packs for completeness and accuracy.
Support clear interface management between construction, QA/QC, commissioning, and operational teams.
Maintain strong safety and quality standards throughout energisation, functional testing, and final handover.

Good knowledge of critical electrical systems and commissioning close-out controls.

jobs.title: Quantity Surveyor — Data Centre Cabling
jobs.customer: Specialist electrical and data cabling contractor
jobs.overview: Quantity Surveyor required to support commercial oversight across data centre cabling projects for a specialist contractor working across HV, LV, data, and fibre installation scopes. The role will suit someone comfortable managing commercial processes across technically led electrical packages, with a focus on cost control, subcontract administration, valuations, variations, forecasting, and project reporting. This is a strong fit for a QS who understands delivery risk, package change, and the level of commercial detail needed to support specialist installation teams in the mission-critical sector.
responsibilities: Support commercial management across data centre cabling and electrical installation packages.
Prepare and review valuations, variations, payment applications, CVRs, and cost reports.
Track subcontractor accounts, procurement activity, change control, and package commercial performance.
Work closely with operations and delivery teams on scope alignment, programme impact, and forecasting.
Maintain accurate commercial records, contractual correspondence, and support final account close-out.
Provide clear reporting on cost movement, risk, and commercial opportunity across live projects.
requirements: Quantity Surveying experience within electrical, MEP, cabling, or infrastructure packages.
Strong commercial administration, reporting, change control, and forecast management capability.
Comfortable working across specialist installation scopes such as HV/LV, structured cabling, or fibre packages.
Good stakeholder communication with operational, delivery, and commercial teams.
Organised approach to records, valuations, contract compliance, and final account support.

shareSpec.overview: HMJ Global is supporting the appointment of a Quantity Surveyor for a specialist contractor delivering data centre cabling and electrical installation works across the UK and wider project markets. This role is centred on commercial control of technically led packages, including HV, LV, data, and fibre-related scopes, with responsibility for supporting valuations, variations, forecasting, subcontract administration, and final account processes. The successful candidate will be someone who is comfortable operating close to delivery teams while maintaining disciplined reporting, record keeping, and package-level commercial visibility. It is particularly well suited to a QS looking to apply strong commercial fundamentals within a specialist electrical and mission-critical project environment.
responsibilities: Support commercial control across specialist cabling and electrical packages.
Prepare valuations, variations, CVRs, payment applications, and cost reports.
Manage subcontractor accounts, change control, and package-level forecasting.
Work closely with operations and delivery teams on scope, procurement, and programme impacts.
Maintain accurate records and contractual documentation through to final account.
Report clearly on cost movement, risk, and opportunity across live projects.
requirements: Quantity Surveying experience in electrical, MEP, cabling, or infrastructure delivery.
Strong understanding of commercial reporting, variations, and subcontract administration.
Comfortable working in site-linked or delivery-focused project environments.
Good communication with operational and commercial stakeholders.
Organised approach to compliance, records, and forecasting accuracy.

jobs.title: Electrical Testing Engineer — Data Centre
jobs.customer: Mission-critical electrical testing and inspection specialist
jobs.overview: Electrical Testing Engineer required to support electrical inspection and testing activities on mission-critical data centre programmes. This permanent opportunity includes accommodation and travel support and will suit someone able to coordinate electricians and engineers on site while maintaining safe, organised, and high-quality testing delivery. The role combines hands-on technical oversight with team coordination, reporting, and close-out support across fast-paced critical infrastructure environments where uptime, electrical integrity, and structured turnover are essential.
responsibilities: Oversee electrical inspection and testing activities across live mission-critical projects.
Coordinate site electricians and engineers to ensure testing works are planned, sequenced, and executed safely.
Review progress, inspections, test records, and close-out actions to support timely completion.
Maintain clear communication with site management and wider delivery stakeholders throughout testing phases.
Support quality, compliance, startup readiness, and structured handover of tested systems.
Promote strong safety standards and orderly workface coordination across inspection, testing, and turnover activities.
requirements: Electrical testing or inspection experience on complex commercial or industrial projects.
Confident leading or coordinating site teams during live testing activities.
Strong understanding of safe electrical work practices, testing records, and documentation close-out.
Experience with critical electrical infrastructure such as switchgear, transformers, breakers, cables, grounding, or associated distribution systems is advantageous.
Able to communicate clearly with site teams and project stakeholders.
Mission-critical or data centre experience is advantageous.

shareSpec.overview: HMJ Global is supporting the search for an Electrical Testing Engineer to join a specialist mission-critical testing environment focused on data centre delivery. This permanent role combines practical electrical inspection and testing support with on-site coordination responsibility, helping ensure systems are checked, recorded, and progressed in a safe and well-structured way. The successful candidate will be comfortable working closely with electricians, engineers, and site management while keeping testing activities organised, maintaining quality standards, and supporting timely close-out. With accommodation and travel support included, this opportunity suits someone who can bring both technical discipline and calm site coordination to fast-paced electrical testing phases across critical infrastructure projects.
responsibilities: Oversee electrical inspection and testing activities on mission-critical project work fronts.
Coordinate electricians and engineers to keep testing works safe, organised, and progressing.
Review test records, progress updates, and close-out actions.
Support startup readiness, quality compliance, and structured handover of tested systems.
Communicate clearly with site management and wider delivery stakeholders.
Maintain strong safety and workface control across inspection and testing phases.
requirements: Electrical testing or inspection experience on complex project environments.
Able to coordinate teams during live testing and close-out activities.
Strong awareness of safe electrical practices and documentation standards.
Comfortable working around critical electrical infrastructure and testing processes.
Good communication skills and a practical, site-focused mindset.
